formal_model_status.ordinary_standard_signal_locus: lines 420-427, ordinary standard-signal high-rail and bit-append behavior
formal_model_status.gap: The formal model gives binary-input behavior for ordinary standard signals and also names standard-signal at command offset 0, but it does not define whether that command token should execute from self mailbox state or preserve the current command boundary.

command_token_binary_input_resolution.summary: The formal model separately names ordinary standard-signal processing and the command-table standard-signal entry, while the reviewed legacy witnesses exclude standard-signal from special-message dispatch and treat ordinary standard input separately.

formal_model_self_mailbox_exception.summary: The formal model says wire, proc, and stem cells perform productive behavior on standard signals unless the standard signal is sent to the self-mailbox of a stem cell. This blocks treating a stem self-mailbox standard-signal command as ordinary binary-input standard-signal behavior.

recipient_surface_resolution.summary: Delivered recipient standard-signal command messages stay in the already-claimed non-init recipient rejection boundary. The formal model gives no recipient execution rule for them, and RAA, SEMSIM, and FSMSIM exclude standard-signal from special-message sets.

self_target_surface_resolution.summary: AS preserves direct self-mailbox standard-signal as unsupported and preserves completed self-target command-buffer standard-signal at the append boundary.

resolved_resolution_questions.legacy_divergence: RAA maps its final command-buffer case to standard-signal, but AS preserves the formal PRC command-buffer map from ADR-0026.

The formal model excludes standard signals sent to the self-mailbox of a stem cell from productive ordinary standard-signal behavior; legacy witnesses exclude standard-signal from special messages and do not provide a matching command-token execution rule.

The formal model provides no recipient execution rule for delivered standard-signal command messages; RAA, SEMSIM, and FSMSIM exclude standard-signal from special-message sets, and AS already claims UC-RECIPIENT-NON-INIT-COMMAND-MESSAGE-REJECTED.

The formal model separately names ordinary standard-signal processing and the command-table standard-signal entry; RAA, SEMSIM, and FSMSIM exclude standard-signal from special-message dispatch and treat ordinary standard input separately.

AS already preserves direct self-mailbox standard-signal under UC-STEM-SELF-MAILBOX-UNSUPPORTED-PRESERVED and preserves completed self-target command-buffer standard-signal under UC-STEM-COMMAND-BUFFER-UNSUPPORTED-APPENDED.

legacy_standard_signal_witnesses.summary: RAA treats standard-signal as the numeric ordinary standard input value and excludes it from special-messages, while its buffer-process table maps the final command case to standard-signal rather than formal offset 0.
SEMSIM defines standard-signal separately from special-messages and classifies binary standard input before ordinary wire/proc/stem standard-signal processing.
FSMSIM excludes standard-signal from special-messages, classifies nonzero binary input as ordinary standard input, and indexes command-buffer messages through the seven special messages only.

additional_source_statuses.summary: guile-asmsim.scm keeps standard signals as ordinary binary input while process-buffer appends numeric standard-signals to its command list; this strengthens the command-token blocker rather than resolving it.

practice/asmsim.scm uses process-buffer code-shape predicates and a tar+sic? helper rather than the formal standard-signal command token, with comments warning that message codes need documentation and confirmation.
The official PRC TLA files are partial/stub/empty and do not define standard-signal command-token semantics.

as_boundary: Continue rejecting or preserving standard-signal command tokens at the existing claimed boundaries. AS already has ordinary standard-signal routing and stem buffer accumulation for binary input; this artifact blocks only command-token execution.
